title: Adicea trianaeana
intro: Adicea trianaeana, also known as the "Santa Barbara sedge," is a species of flowering plant in the sedge family, belonging to the genus Adicea. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate symptoms of conditions such as fever, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Adicea trianaeana include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Adicea trianaeana is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as being used in the form of a salve or ointment to treat skin conditions and wounds.
